Upper zones of fire suppresion system complete
Either permanent or temporary personnel gowning area (s) established for entry intyo cleanroom construction zone
Established permanent or temporary equipment staging room established for production of equipment into clean zone
Stage 3 - Cleanroom Walls, Ceiling, and Floors Complete
Laminate floor material laid and sealed
Fire suppression system installed and tested
TGM alarm system installed and tested
Make Up air ductwork complete and tested
Housekeeping vacuum system complete and tested
FFUs with ULPA filters installed with pre-filters. FFU are not to be operating at the stage, only in place
Stage 4 - Initial Cleanroom Certification; As-Built Status Achieved
Floor, ceiling and wall changes complete
Protective covers removed from floor, ceiling and walls
Fire suppression system charged and operable
Recirculation air system operable and adjusted
Make Up air system operable and adjusted
Ceiling grid and HEPA/ ULPA scan
All permanent cleanroom doors installed and operable at all levels
Contamination level stable within project ISO specifications
Stage 5 - Intermediate and Final Qualification of Cleanroom and Installation of Production Equipment; Operational Status Achieved.
All control systems tested and callibrated
Contamination level stabilized within project ISO specifications
Final cleanroom certification signed off by site Contamination Control

Supply, fabrication, installation, testing, and commissioning of above-ground mild steel (MS) piping system for Internal hydratn system. The work includes but is not limited to:
MS ERW pipes (IS 1239 Heavy Class up to 150 NB, IS 3589 ≥150 NB, min. 6.35 mm thick)
Pipe jointing by grooved couplings (≥50 NB) and threaded joints (≤50 NB), including gaskets, bolts, adapters, reducers, mechanical tees, and end caps (UL/FM approved)
Hydrostatic testing at 1.5 times working pressure (IS 13039 / NFPA 13)
Radiographic testing of welded joints (if any fabrication welds exist, min. 10% joints, 50% of which shall be field joints)
Flushing, commissioning, and handover including as-built documentation as per technical specification
Providing and applying approved fire sealant at all locations where pipes penetrate walls, complete as per specifications

Material: Body – Ductile Iron (DI); Trim & Internal Components – Stainless Steel (SS304/SS316) / Brass; Seat & Seals – EPDM / NBR (suitable for fire water applications)
Workng Pressure : 175 PSI min, Hydrostatic Test Pressure: As per UL/FM Standards
Coating: Fusion Bonded Epoxy (FBE) coated (inside & outside) as per AWWA C550
Operation: Permits flow of water in one direction and actuates alarm upon system activation. Assembly equipped with Pressure Switch, Water Motor Gong (Mechanical Alarm Bell), and Retarding Chamber to

Accessories: Alarm Pressure Switch, Water Motor Gong, Retarding Chamber, Drain Valve & Pressure Gauges, Trim Set (including isolation valves, strainers, and test connections), nuts, bolts, and gaskets
Type: Vertical Bladder Tank with Proportioning Assembly (balanced pressure type)

Material of Construction: Tank Shell: Carbon Steel, internally epoxy-coated for corrosion resistance, Bladder: High-grade elastomer, suitable for fluorine-free foam concentrates, Piping & Trim: Epoxy-
Design Pressure: Minimum 175 PSI (12 bar) or higher, UL/FM rated
End Connections: Flanged, ANSI Class 150 / PN16
Coating: External – Epoxy paint system, UV resistant; Internal – Food-grade epoxy lining (for foam concentrate compatibility)
Foam Compatibility: Fully compatible with fluorine-free foam concentrates (Class A and Class B applications, as specified), resistant to environmental stress cracking
Accessories: Proportioner (ratio controller), isolation valves, pressure gauges, vent valve, drain valve, relief valve, foam concentrate inlet/outlet, and trim piping complete in all respects
Operation: Balanced pressure proportioning system to ensure accurate foam-water ratio across full operating range.
Temperature Stability: 0 °C to +55 °C (storage & use as per manufacturer’s data sheet)
Shelf Life: Minimum 10 years under proper storage conditions
Body Material: Corrosion-Resistant Brass / Stainless Steel (SS 304 / SS 316)
Switch Type: SPDT (Single Pole Double Throw) Contact
Flow Sensitivity: Adjustable (as per site conditions)
Installation Location: Fire sprinkler piping / Hydrant mains / Fire pump discharge

Supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of UL Listed & FM Approved Test and Drain Valve for fire protection piping systems, complete with all necessary accessories such as nuts, bolts, gasket
Type: Integrated Ball Valve with Test and Drain Function
Material: Body: Forged Brass, Ball: Stainless Steel (SS304 / SS316) with Chrome Plating, Stem: Stainless Steel (SS304 / SS316), Seat: PTFE / RPTFE (Fire-Safe Design)
End Connection: Threaded (as per IS 554 / ANSI B1.20.1) or Flanged (as per IS 6392 / ANSI B16.5 Class 150)
Operation: Handwheel / Lever Operated with Drain & Test Function, Adjustable Orifice for Flow Control, Integrated Sight Glass for Flow Indication
Accessories: Nuts, Bolts, Gaskets, and Locking Arrangement (if applicable)
Air Vent Assembly: Brass / Stainless Steel body, float-operated, maintenance-free, corrosion resistant, working pressure up to 300 PSI, connection size ½″ NPT/BSPT.

Ball Valve Supervisory Switch: Potter RBVS-T or equivalent UL/FM approved retrofit switch, SPDT contacts rated 10 A @ 125/250 VAC, enclosure NEMA 4 weatherproof, suitable for ½″–2″ valves, with tamper
Approvals: UL Listed & FM Approved for both air vent and supervisory switch.
Operation: Automatic air vent function with float mechanism; supervisory switch generates alarm signal if valve handle is moved from normal (open) position.
Accessories: Includes nipples, unions, clamps, supports, and mounting hardware for complete installation.
Standard of Reference: NFPA 13 (Air Vent Requirements), NFPA 72 (Supervisory Signals), UL/FM standards.
Material: Body – Brass, chrome plated; Deflector – Stainless Steel (SS304 / SS316); Frame & Components – corrosion-resistant materials
Type: Foam-Water Sprinkler (Pendant / Upright type) with internal foam-water mixing capability as per system design.
Working Pressure: Minimum 175 PSI (12 bar) or higher as per system design
Sprinkler guard shall be supplied along with the sprinkler.
Pendant / Upright type, 68 degree, K 160, Quick response, Sprinkler Escutcheon Plates
Material: Heavy-duty corrosion-resistant stainless steel (SS304 or SS316)
Type: Cage-type guard suitable for pendant and upright sprinklers
Compatibility: Suitable for foam sprinkler system operation

Foam System Requirement: For foam-water sprinkler systems, sprinkler guards shall be specifically UL Listed / FM Approved by the sprinkler manufacturer for foam service and shall not obstruct or adver
Accessories: Complete with mounting hardware and brackets
Sprinkler guard shall be supplied from the same manufacturer as the sprinkler head
Connection: ½″ BSP/NPT threaded, complying with IS 3624 or equivalent UL/FM standard
Accessories: Gauge shall be provided with shut-off valve, siphon, unions, adaptors, and mounting brackets for secure installation
Design: Corrosion-resistant, calibrated, and designed for accurate pressure monitoring in fire protection service
Scope: Includes supply, installation, calibration, and testing to ensure proper functionality in the fire protection system

C - FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEM - GASIOUS SYSTEM

Design, Supply, Installation, Testing & Commissioning (SITC) of an Natural Gas-Based Fire Fighting System (using inert gases such as IG-541 ), UL/FM approved, fully compliant with NFPA 2001 (latest ed
The system shall be designed as a clean, environmentally friendly, and human-safe fire suppression solution and shall include but not be limited to the following components:

Design & Engineering - Agent quantity calculation and complete hydraulic flow analysis as per NFPA 2001, System design to ensure extinguishing concentration is achieved within 60 seconds or as per NFP

Alarm & Warning Devices - Audio-Visual Pre-discharge Alarm: Hooters and Flashing Strobes inside and outside protected rooms, Discharge Indicator Lamps, Pressure Switches for cylinder discharge confirm
Accessories & Trim - Check valves, isolation valves, pressure relief valves, strainers, gauges, and fittings as required, All supporting materials, grooved couplings, brackets, and hangers.

Testing & Commissioning - Room Integrity Test (Door Fan Test) to verify enclosure tightness and hold time requirements, Functional testing of detection, alarm, control, and manual activation features,
Compliance & Approvals - Cylinders shall be PESO approved (India statutory requirement)., All components shall be UL Listed / FM Approved, System designed, installed, and tested as per NFPA 2001 and
CUB1, MCC Room ( 32 M X 10 M X 5 M) and Battery Room( 5M X 4 M X 5 M approx). The final design shall be based on the actual room size.
FAB 1, UPS Room ( 8.3 M X 4.7 M X 7.5 M). The final design shall be based on the actual room size.
High-pressure inert gas cylinder (IG-541), seamless steel, PESO approved, 300-bar working pressure, complete with identification label and protective cap.
1.2.1 - UL/FM approved cylinder valve assembly for IG-541, complete with safety burst disc, pressure gauge, and valve outlet fittings.
1.2.2 - Electrical actuator (solenoid) for cylinder valve, 24 VDC, UL/FM listed, complete with diode and mounting accessories.
1.2.3 - Pneumatic actuator for cylinder valve, UL/FM listed, complete with tubing and fittings.
1.2.4 - Manual actuator / manual release device for cylinder valve, UL/FM listed.
1.2.5 - Constant-flow / pressure-regulating device to limit discharge pressure to system design value (max. 60 bar), UL/FM listed.
1.2.6 - Flexible high-pressure discharge hose with non-return valve (NRV), UL/FM approved, suitable for IG-541 service.
1.2.7 - Cylinder valve supervisory / monitoring switch for valve status indication.
1.2.8 - Floor-mounted steel cylinder rack / support frame with anchoring accessories and protective cage, suitable for discharge and seismic loads.
1.2.1 - Pilot / control gas cylinder, PESO & UL/FM approved, complete with valve and accessories.
1.2.2 - Pressure reducer / regulator for pilot gas system, UL/FM listed.
1.2.3 - Pilot hose with fittings and O-rings, UL/FM approved.
1.2.4 - Shuttle non-return valve (NRV) for pilot line, UL/FM approved.
1.2.5 - Bleed valve / safety valve for pilot line protection.
1.2.6 - Supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of reset tool for electromagnetic actuator, complete with protective cap and associated accessories.
1.2.7 - Pressure gauge for pilot gas cylinder and pilot line.
1.3.2 - Providing and applying approved fire sealant at all locations where pipes penetrate walls, complete as per specifications
1.3.3 - Design, Supply and Installation of Modular pipe support system / fabricated steel supports with anchors and fasteners.
1.3.4 - Discharge nozzles, UL/FM approved, 360° / 180° type, sized as per approved hydraulic calculations.
1.3.6 - Pressure switch / flow switch for discharge confirmation, UL/FM approved.
1.3.7 - Pressure relief device for discharge manifold, UL/FM approved.
1.4.1 - Selector valve, UL/FM approved, suitable for IG-541 discharge system (where multi-zone protection is required).
1.4.2 - Manual selector valve operating lever with accessories.
1.4.3 - Pneumatic selector valve control unit with control gas accessories.
1.5.1 - Fire-rated pressure relief damper for protected enclosure, complete with calculation, installation, and testing.
1.6 - FIRE DETECTION & GAS RELEASE CONTROL SYSTEM
1.6.1 - Gas Release Control Panel, UL/FM listed, compliant with NFPA 72 & NFPA 2001, complete with minimum 24-hour battery backup.
1.6.2 - Abort-cum-Manual Release Station, UL/FM approved, including wiring to the gas release control panel.
1.6.3 - Audible alarm (hooter/horn) for pre-discharge and discharge warning, 24 VDC, including cabling and installation.
1.6.4 - Visual alarm (strobe) for pre-discharge and discharge warning, 24 VDC, including cabling and installation.

1.6.5 - Public Address (PA) speaker for evacuation announcements inside protected areas, complete with back box, mounting accessories, and cabling, integrated with the fire alarm / gas release logic a
1.6.6 - Evacuation indicator light with illuminated message “EVACUATE IMMEDIATELY”, UL/FM approved, including cabling and installation.
1.6.7 - Gas discharge indicator light with illuminated message “GAS DISCHARGE – DO NOT ENTER”, UL/FM approved, including cabling and installation.
1.6.8 - Fire-rated control cabling (2-core × 1.5 sq.mm), conduits, junction boxes, terminations, testing, and identification
1.7.1 - Surface preparation and painting of exposed piping and steel supports (SA 2½, epoxy primer + fire-red epoxy finish).
1.8.1 - Door Fan (Enclosure Integrity) Test in accordance with NFPA 2001, including certified report.
1.9.1 - Actual IG-541 discharge test in accordance with NFPA 2001, including post-discharge reinstatement and test report.

2 - If additional Support required same need to be considered by General contractor. Its Design and Validation shall be done before fabrication and installation.
3 - All Unistrut support shall be epoxy coated and expoxy coating colour code shall follow project specification.
4 - Unistrut Specification and design ( single or Double) need to follow global standard, Drawing and 3D model.

5 - This pipe Support shall be read in conjuction with MEP support Standard. (Client Doc. No: A-MC-MSI-1-TP-CP-PM-SP-010) and with Gas Support Drawing (Client Doc No- DE-MSI1-FAB-B-001-N-408, ,DE-MSI1-CUB-B-001-N-409 and DE-MSI1-FAB-B-001-N-413).
